63_1_1.NKJV: The Elder, To the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth, and not only I, but also all those who have known the truth,

63_1_1.NASB: The elder to the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in truth; and not only I, but also all who know the truth,

63_1_1.CSB17: The elder: To the elect <sup>ⓘ</sup> lady and her children, whom I love in the truth — and not only I, but also all who know the truth <sup>ⓙ</sup>  —

The elder,
To the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in the truth—and not I only, but also all who know the truth—
63_1_1.NRSVCE: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in the truth, and not only I but also all who know the truth,

63_1_2.NKJV: because of the truth which abides in us and will be with us forever:

63_1_2.NRSVCE: because of the truth that abides in us and will be with us forever:

63_1_3.NKJV: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with you from God the Father and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

63_1_3.ESV: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us, from God the Father and from Jesus Christ the Father's Son, in truth and love.
63_1_3.NASB: Grace, mercy [and] peace will be with us, from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.
63_1_3.NLT: Grace, mercy, and peace, which come from God the Father and from Jesus Christ — the Son of the Father — will continue to be with us who live in truth and love.

63_1_3.CSB17: Grace, mercy, and peace <sup>ⓛ</sup> will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the Father, in truth and love. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
63_1_3.AMP: Grace, mercy, and peace (inner calm, a sense of spiritual well-being) will be with us, from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Father's Son, in truth and love.

63_1_3.NIV: Grace, mercy and peace from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Father’s Son, will be with us in truth and love.
63_1_3.NRSVCE: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Father’s Son, in truth and love.

63_1_4.NKJV: I rejoiced greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, as we received commandment from the Father.
63_1_4.KJV: I rejoiced<S>5463</S> greatly<S>3029</S> that<S>3754</S> I found<S>2147</S> of<S>1537</S> thy<S>4675</S> children<S>5043</S> walking<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> truth<S>225</S>, as<S>2531</S> we have received<S>2983</S> a commandment<S>1785</S> from<S>3844</S> the Father<S>3962</S>.
63_1_4.ESV: I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we were commanded by the Father.
63_1_4.NASB: I was very glad to find [some] of your children walking in truth, just as we have received commandment [to do] from the Father.
63_1_4.NLT: How happy I was to meet some of your children and find them living according to the truth, just as the Father commanded.
63_1_4.WEB: I rejoice greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, even as we have been commanded by the Father.
63_1_4.CSB17: I was very glad to find some of your children walking in truth, <sup>ⓐ</sup> in keeping with a command we have received from the Father.
63_1_4.AMP: I was greatly delighted to find some of your children walking in truth, just as we have been commanded by the Father.
63_1_4.MSG: I can't tell you how happy I am to learn that many members of your congregation are diligent in living out the Truth, exactly as commanded by the Father.
63_1_4.NIV: It has given me great joy to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as the Father commanded us.
63_1_4.NRSVCE: I was overjoyed to find some of your children walking in the truth, just as we have been commanded by the Father.

63_1_5.NKJV: And now I plead with you, lady, not as though I wrote a new commandment to you, but that which we have had from the beginning: that we love one another.
63_1_5.KJV: And<S>2532</S> now<S>3568</S> I beseech<S>2065</S> thee<S>4571</S>, lady<S>2959</S>, not<S>3756</S> as though<S>5613</S> I wrote<S>1125</S> <S>1125</S> a new<S>2537</S> commandment<S>1785</S> unto thee<S>4671</S>, but<S>235</S> that which<S>3739</S> we had<S>2192</S> from<S>575</S> the beginning<S>746</S>, that<S>2443</S> we love<S>25</S> one another<S>240</S>.
63_1_5.ESV: And now I ask you, dear lady — not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning — that we love one another.
63_1_5.NASB: Now I ask you, lady, not as though [I were] writing to you a new commandment, but the one which we have had from the beginning, that we love one another.
63_1_5.NLT: I am writing to remind you, dear friends, that we should love one another. This is not a new commandment, but one we have had from the beginning.
63_1_5.WEB: Now I beg you, dear lady, not as though I wrote to you a new commandment, but that which we had from the beginning, that we love one another.
63_1_5.CSB17: So now I ask you, dear lady — not as if I were writing you a new command, but one we have had from the beginning — that we love one another. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
63_1_5.AMP: Now I ask you, lady, not as if I were writing to you a new commandment, but [simply reminding you of] the one which we have had from the beginning, that we love and unselfishly seek the best for one another.
63_1_5.MSG: But permit me a reminder, friends, and this is not a new commandment but simply a repetition of our original and basic charter: that we love each other.
63_1_5.NIV: And now, dear lady, I am not writing you a new command but one we have had from the beginning. I ask that we love one another.
63_1_5.NRSVCE: But now, dear lady, I ask you, not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but one we have had from the beginning, let us love one another.

63_1_6.NKJV: This is love, that we walk according to His commandments. This is the commandment, that as you have heard from the beginning, you should walk in it.
63_1_6.KJV: And<S>2532</S> this<S>3778</S> is<S>2076</S> love<S>26</S>, that<S>2443</S> we walk<S>4043</S> after<S>2596</S> his<S>846</S> commandments<S>1785</S>. This<S>3778</S> is<S>2076</S> the commandment<S>1785</S>, That<S>2443</S>, as<S>2531</S> ye have heard<S>191</S> from<S>575</S> the beginning<S>746</S>, ye should walk<S>4043</S> in<S>1722</S> it<S>846</S>.
63_1_6.ESV: And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments; this is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, so that you should walk in it.
63_1_6.NASB: And this is love, that we walk according to His commandments. This is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
63_1_6.NLT: Love means doing what God has commanded us, and he has commanded us to love one another, just as you heard from the beginning.
63_1_6.WEB: This is love, that we should walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, even as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
63_1_6.CSB17: This is love: <sup>ⓒ</sup> that we walk according to his commands. <sup>ⓓ</sup> This is the command as you have heard it from the beginning: that you walk in love. <sup>[1]</sup>
63_1_6.AMP: And this is love: that we walk in accordance with His commandments and are guided continually by His precepts. This is the commandment, just as you have heard from the beginning, that you should [always] walk in love.
63_1_6.MSG: Love means following his commandments, and his unifying commandment is that you conduct your lives in love. This is the first thing you heard, and nothing has changed.
63_1_6.NIV: And this is love: that we walk in obedience to his commands. As you have heard from the beginning, his command is that you walk in love.
63_1_6.NRSVCE: And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments; this is the commandment just as you have heard it from the beginning — you must walk in it.

63_1_7.NKJV: For many deceivers have gone out into the world who do not confess Jesus Christ as coming in the flesh. This is a deceiver and an antichrist.

This<S>3778</S> is<S>2076</S> a deceiver<S>4108</S> and<S>2532</S> an antichrist<S>500</S>.
63_1_7.ESV: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh. Such a one is the deceiver and the antichrist.
63_1_7.NASB: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not acknowledge Jesus Christ [as] coming in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.
63_1_7.NLT: I say this because many deceivers have gone out into the world. They deny that Jesus Christ came in a real body. Such a person is a deceiver and an antichrist.
63_1_7.WEB: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who don’t confess that Jesus Christ came in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the Antichrist.
63_1_7.CSB17: Many deceivers have gone out into the world; they do not confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh. <sup>ⓔ</sup> This is the deceiver and the antichrist. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
63_1_7.AMP: For many deceivers [heretics, posing as Christians] have gone out into the world, those who do not acknowledge and confess the coming of Jesus Christ in the flesh (bodily form). This [person, the kind who does this] is the deceiver and the antichrist [that is, the antagonist of Christ].
63_1_7.MSG: There are a lot of smooth-talking charlatans loose in the world who refuse to believe that Jesus Christ was truly human, a flesh-and-blood human being. Give them their true title: Deceiver! Antichrist!
63_1_7.NIV: Many deceivers, who do not acknowledge Jesus Christ as coming in the flesh, have gone out into the world. Any such person is the deceiver and the antichrist.
63_1_7.NRSVCE: Many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who do not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh; any such person is the deceiver and the antichrist!

63_1_8.NKJV: Look to yourselves, that we do not lose those things we worked for, but that we may receive a full reward.

63_1_8.ESV: Watch yourselves, so that you may not lose what we  have worked for, but may win a full reward.
63_1_8.NASB: Watch yourselves, that you do not lose what we have accomplished, but that you may receive a full reward.
63_1_8.NLT: Watch out that you do not lose what we have worked so hard to achieve. Be diligent so that you receive your full reward.
63_1_8.WEB: Watch yourselves, that we don’t lose the things which we have accomplished, but that we receive a full reward.
63_1_8.CSB17: Watch yourselves so you don’t lose what we <sup>[2]</sup> have worked for, but that you may receive a full reward. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
63_1_8.AMP: Watch yourselves, so that you do not lose what we have accomplished together, but that you may receive a full and perfect reward [when He grants rewards to faithful believers].
63_1_8.MSG: And be very careful around them so you don't lose out on what we've worked so diligently in together; I want you to get every reward you have coming to you.
63_1_8.NIV: Watch out that you do not lose what you have worked for, but that you may be rewarded fully.
63_1_8.NRSVCE: Be on your guard, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but may receive a full reward.

63_1_9.NKJV: Whoever transgresses and does not abide in the doctrine of Christ does not have God. He who abides in the doctrine of Christ has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.KJV: Whosoever<S>3956</S> transgresseth<S>3845</S>, and<S>2532</S> abideth<S>3306</S> not<S>3361</S> in<S>1722</S> the doctrine<S>1322</S> of Christ<S>5547</S>, hath<S>2192</S> not<S>3756</S> God<S>2316</S>. He that abideth<S>3306</S> in<S>1722</S> the doctrine<S>1322</S> of Christ<S>5547</S>, he<S>3778</S> hath<S>2192</S> both<S>2532</S> the Father<S>3962</S> and<S>2532</S> the Son<S>5207</S>.
63_1_9.ESV: Everyone who goes on ahead and does not abide in the teaching of Christ, does not have God. Whoever abides in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.NASB: Anyone who goes too far and does not abide in the teaching of Christ, does not have God; the one who abides in the teaching, he has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.NLT: Anyone who wanders away from this teaching has no relationship with God. But anyone who remains in the teaching of Christ has a relationship with both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.WEB: Whoever transgresses and doesn’t remain in the teaching of Christ, doesn’t have God. He who remains in the teaching, the same has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.CSB17: Anyone who does not remain in Christ’s teaching <sup>ⓗ</sup> but goes beyond it does not have God. <sup>ⓘ</sup> The one who remains <sup>ⓙ</sup> in that teaching, this one has both the Father <sup>ⓚ</sup> and the Son. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
63_1_9.AMP: Anyone who runs on ahead and does not remain in the doctrine of Christ [that is, one who is not content with what He taught], does not have God; but the one who continues to remain in the teaching [of Christ does have God], he has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.MSG: Anyone who gets so progressive in his thinking that he walks out on the teaching of Christ, walks out on God. But whoever stays with the teaching, stays faithful to both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.NIV: Anyone who runs ahead and does not continue in the teaching of Christ does not have God; whoever continues in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.
63_1_9.NRSVCE: Everyone who does not abide in the teaching of Christ, but goes beyond it, does not have God; whoever abides in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.

63_1_10.NKJV: If anyone comes to you and does not bring this doctrine, do not receive him into your house nor greet him;
63_1_10.KJV: If<S>1536</S> there come<S>2064</S> any<S>1536</S> <S>5100</S> unto<S>4314</S> you<S>5209</S>, and<S>2532</S> bring<S>5342</S> not<S>3756</S> this<S>5026</S> doctrine<S>1322</S>, receive<S>2983</S> him<S>846</S> not<S>3361</S> into<S>1519</S> your house<S>3614</S>, neither<S>2532</S> <S>3361</S> bid<S>3004</S> him<S>846</S> God speed<S>5463</S>:

63_1_10.NLT: If anyone comes to your meeting and does not teach the truth about Christ, don’t invite that person into your home or give any kind of encouragement.
63_1_10.WEB: If anyone comes to you, and doesn’t bring this teaching, don’t receive him into your house, and don’t welcome him,

63_1_11.ESV: for whoever greets him takes part in his wicked works.

63_1_12.NKJV: Having many things to write to you, I did not wish to do so with paper and ink; but I hope to come to you and speak face to face, that our joy may be full.
63_1_12.KJV: Having<S>2192</S> many things<S>4183</S> to write<S>1125</S> unto you<S>5213</S>, I would<S>1014</S> not<S>3756</S> write with<S>1223</S> paper<S>5489</S> and<S>2532</S> ink<S>3188</S>: but<S>235</S> I trust<S>1679</S> to come<S>2064</S> unto<S>4314</S> you<S>5209</S>, and<S>2532</S> speak<S>2980</S> face<S>4750</S> to<S>4314</S> face<S>4750</S>, that<S>2443</S> our<S>2257</S> joy<S>5479</S> may be<S>5600</S> full<S>4137</S>. <sup>face to: Gr. mouth to mouth</sup> <sup>our: or, your</sup>
63_1_12.ESV: Though I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink. Instead I hope to come to you and talk face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
63_1_12.NASB: Though I have many things to write to you, I do not want to [do so] with paper and ink; but I hope to come to you and speak face to face, so that your joy may be made full.
63_1_12.NLT: I have much more to say to you, but I don’t want to do it with paper and ink. For I hope to visit you soon and talk with you face to face. Then our joy will be complete.
63_1_12.WEB: Having many things to write to you, I don’t want to do so with paper and ink, but I hope to come to you, and to speak face to face, that our joy may be made full.
63_1_12.CSB17: Though I have many things to write to you, <sup>ⓑ</sup> I don’t want to use paper and ink. Instead, I hope to come to you and talk face to face so that our joy <sup>ⓒ</sup> may be complete.
63_1_12.AMP: I have many things to write to you, but I prefer not to do so with paper (papyrus) and black (ink); but I hope to come to you and speak with you face to face, so that your joy may be complete.
63_1_12.MSG: I have a lot more things to tell you, but I'd rather not use paper and ink. I hope to be there soon in person and have a heart-to-heart talk. That will be far more satisfying to both you and me.
63_1_12.NIV: I have much to write to you, but I do not want to use paper and ink. Instead, I hope to visit you and talk with you face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
63_1_12.NRSVCE: Although I have much to write to you, I would rather not use paper and ink; instead I hope to come to you and talk with you face to face, so that our joy may be complete.
